evals.description: Standard blog crawl with fetch_posts false — archive-level data only
assertions: Returns valid JSON without a top-level error field
site_url matches the input url
archive_url is the discovered blog section URL
inventory_date is present in YYYY-MM-DD format
total_posts_found is a non-negative integer
posts_inventoried is a non-negative integer <= max_posts (30)
fetch_posts_used is false
posts array contains entries, each with title, url, publish_date, estimated_word_count, word_count_source, excerpt, categories, and tags fields
word_count_source for each post is 'estimated_from_excerpt' or 'unavailable' when fetch_posts is false
h2_headings and internal_link_count are null for all posts when fetch_posts is false
summary object contains posting_frequency_per_month, date_range, top_categories, top_tags, content_depth_distribution, avg_estimated_word_count, and freshness_pct fields
content_depth_distribution has under_500, short, medium, long, and unknown keys
Output contains no content strategy recommendations

evals.description: With fetch_posts true — headings and internal link counts populated
assertions: Returns valid JSON without a top-level error field
fetch_posts_used is true
posts_inventoried is <= 5 (max_posts cap enforced)
For posts that were successfully fetched, h2_headings is an array (not null)
For posts that were successfully fetched, internal_link_count is a non-negative integer (not null)
For posts that could not be fetched individually, h2_headings and internal_link_count are null (not a failure)

evals.description: Site with no detectable content archive — returns structured error
assertions: If no content archive is found, returns JSON with an error field
error value is 'no_content_archive_found' or 'fetch_failed'
url field is present in the error response
Does not fabricate post listings when none exist
